task:
Consolidate ALL machine-local run-dir path construction behind ONE helper, with ZERO behavior change (the on-disk layout stays exactly flat `local/runs/<runId>` for now — do NOT introduce the nested `<workspaceId>/<runId>` layout in this atom; that is a separate founder-gated decision).

BACKGROUND (the ticket's premise is partly STALE — trust this, re-verified against the live tree, not the ticket text): there is currently NO `localRunDir` helper anywhere and NO `packages/core/src/run-dir.ts` and NO `packages/core/src/retention/gc.ts`. Every run-dir path is built inline. Do not look for a pre-existing helper to extend; create it.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Create a single helper that is the SOLE owner of how a run's machine-local directory path is built from `runsRoot` and a run's identity. Suggested home: `packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts` (export from the package index so daemon/cli can import it). Signature should carry the run's WORKSPACE id as well as its run id even though flat layout ignores the workspace id today — e.g. `localRunDir(runsRoot: string, run: { workspaceId: string; id: string }): string` returning `join(runsRoot, run.id)`. The whole point: the §ADR-0027-§6 layout (flat vs nested `<workspaceId>/<runId>`) must become a change to ONLY this function later. Add a short comment stating that invariant and that flat is the current (unmigrated) layout per ADR-0027 §6 Migration step 5.
2. If any call site has only a run-id STRING and not the workspace id (the resume-pickup reads), DO NOT re-inline `join(runsRoot, id)`. Add a sibling in the SAME module (e.g. `localRunDirById(runsRoot, runId)`) implemented in terms of the same layout so there is still exactly one place the layout is defined. Prefer passing the workspace id through where it is cheaply in scope; only fall back to the by-id form where the workspace id genuinely is not available yet.
3. Route EVERY one of these six live sites through the helper — after this atom, `grep -rn "runsRoot" packages --include=*.ts | grep -v .test. | grep 'join('` must show NO per-run `join(runsRoot, <id>...)` except inside the new helper module and the runsRoot ROOT construction in `packages/daemon/src/server.ts:148` / `packages/cli/src/run.ts:443` (those build `runsRoot` itself — leave them):
   - `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts:386` `const runDir = join(runsRoot, run.id)` (the writer; `run` has `workspaceId`)
   - `packages/daemon/src/oz-context-pointer.ts:92` `join(dirs.runsRoot, run.id)` (`run` has `workspaceId`)
   - `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:1040` `join(ctx.runsRoot, runId, 'oz-nudge.json')` (the live `run` has `workspaceId` in scope just above at the 409 check)
   - `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:220` `join(ctx.runsRoot, opts.resumeFromRunId, 'pickup.md')` (workspace is resolved at line 216 — pass its id, else use the by-id form)
   - `packages/daemon/src/rundir.ts:31` `const dir = join(runsRoot, runId)` (reader; `readRunDir(runsRoot, runId)` — thread workspace id from its daemon caller if cheap, otherwise by-id form)
   - `packages/cli/src/run.ts:500` `join(runsRoot, resumeFromRunId, 'pickup.md')` (resume read; by-id form is fine)
   For sites that append a filename (`pickup.md`, `oz-nudge.json`), do `join(localRunDir(...), 'pickup.md')`.

MUST NOT BREAK / ACCEPTANCE:
- ZERO on-disk behavior change: existing flat `local/runs/<runId>` dirs are still written and read at the identical paths. No migration, no move, no symlink, no ADR edit in this atom.
- Exactly one module owns the layout; no inline per-run join survives outside it (verify with the grep above and report its output).
- Do not touch the ADR, do not change `server.ts:148` / `run.ts:443` runsRoot roots, do not alter any unrelated logic.
- Build + typecheck the whole repo green, and run the existing runner/daemon test suites that touch run dirs (e.g. the runner.ts, launcher, rundir tests). Report the actual commands, exit codes, and the grep output as evidence.

Write-scope: packages/core/src/**, packages/daemon/src/**, packages/cli/src/** (plus the new helper file). Keep the diff minimal and matching surrounding style.
